g.text: bind :: Cont r a -> (a -> Cont r b) -> Cont r b
callCC :: ((a -> Cont r b) -> Cont r a) -> Cont r a

div.p: A Cont r a value is a function that takes a continuation k :: a -> r and returns the final result r.
pure x = \k -> k x — pass x directly to the continuation.
bind m f = \k -> m (\a -> f a k) — give m a continuation that first runs f a, which is then given the original k.
callCC captures the current continuation as an escape function, enabling early exit, exceptions, and coroutines as pure values.

g.title: bind m f = k -> m (a -> f a k). Composes continuations

g.text: f :: (a -> Cont r b) -> Cont r a

g.title: Capture the current continuation k as an escape function. Calling escape(a) jumps directly to the runCont call site with value a.
bind m f = k -> m (a -> f a k). Composes continuations
Capture the current continuation k as an escape function. Calling escape(a) jumps directly to the runCont call site with value a.
